mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) across a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, it signals a deteriorating labor market and weakening consumer spending capacity, which suppresses corporate earnings growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during economic contractions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real auto sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer balance sheets and credit availability, which amplifies revenue growth for cyclical sectors and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side momentum and factory utilization gains, which translates into short-term positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns during early-cycle expansions, or the signal is completely subsumed by monetary policy shocks in event studies.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation readings exceed 235.0 while the federal funds rate remains near zero, the negative real interest rate environment triggers expectations of aggressive future central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive 60-day returns in over 70 percent of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for term premium movements in a 4-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month changes in total nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate household income and consumption spending, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant predictive relationship with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple business cycles, or the signal reverses direction during periods of wage-price spirals.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth decelerates below 1.5 percent, it signals a broad-based economic slowdown that reduces near-term corporate profit expectations, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5 percent, or the threshold effect disappears when adjusted for inventory buildup in a 3-year rolling validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a synchronized supply-labor expansion, which validates sustainable earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when the two series diverge, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate model over a 5-year out-of-sample period.
